priority_order: Implement the first LLM-generated candidate as a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM optimized_lora.cu modeled after /workspace/lora/3_sgemm.cu, not ATen mm and not custom kernels.
Use the exact required row-major/column-major SGEMM mapping: gemm(N,N,d,d,d, X,W -> Y), gemm(N,T,d,16,d, X,B -> U), gemm(N,N,d,d,16, U,A -> Y, beta=1).
After correctness passes for d=3584, 4096, and 4608, benchmark against the current ATen baseline and promote only if aggregate speedup improves.
llm_advice.rationale: The only tested candidate is an ATen reference path: it is correct but slower overall and still performs baseline-style GEMM/add decomposition with explicit transpose/copy overhead. The next required search step is the low-risk pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM family. It preserves cuBLAS for the dominant W @ X GEMM, avoids B.T materialization, computes U as d x 16 via transpose flags, and accumulates the rank-16 update directly into Y with beta=1, reducing launch and ATen overhead while maintaining correctness across all runtime d values.
risk_controls: Require W, X, A, and B to be CUDA float32 tensors on the same device.
Require all inputs to be contiguous with TORCH_CHECK; do not call .contiguous() in the hot path.
Validate shapes: W and X are d x d, A and B are d x 16, and d is in [3584, 4608].
Use at::cuda::CUDAGuard for W.device() and at::cuda::getCurrentCUDABlasHandle() for the current PyTorch CUDA stream.
Allocate Y = torch::empty_like(W) and U = torch::empty({d, 16}, W.options()).
Do not use ATen matmul/mm/add for the main computation.
Do not explicitly materialize B.T or perform transpose-copy operations.
Do not add custom CUDA kernels or explicit cublasSetStream/getCurrentCUDAStream plumbing for this candidate.
Check every cublasSgemm return status with TORCH_CHECK before returning Y.
Treat correctness as a hard gate; never promote this candidate unless all tested d values pass numerical validation.
stop_conditions: Stop and reject if static review finds ATen mm/matmul/add used for the core computation.
Stop and reject if the candidate materializes B.T with transpose().contiguous() or any equivalent explicit copy.
Stop and reject if any cuBLAS SGEMM dimensions, transpose flags, or leading dimensions differ from the required three-SGEMM mapping without strong correctness evidence.
Stop and reject if compilation fails.
Stop and reject if correctness fails for any tested d in the runtime range.
If correct but aggregate speedup is not better than the current best, keep the existing baseline and only then consider low-risk follow-up mutations.

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
